title: Nephrodium tenuifolium
intro: Nephrodium tenuifolium, also known as Lady fern or Five-lobed Lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium tenuifolium include flavonoids, saponins, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Nephrodium tenuifolium is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried or powdered forms for use in capsules or topical applications.
taxonomy: Nephrodium tenuifolium is classified within the division Pteridophyta, which includes ferns and their allies. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores.
